findings.title: CP00-125 README section ends with stale CP00-124 handoff bullet; omits its own CP00-126 handoff
findings.evidence: The new '## CP00-125 ...' section is inserted directly above the pre-existing bullet '- Handoff is fixed to CP00-125 / `RP02.P07.M03.S11`.' (a CP00-124 context line, no '+'). That bullet now trails the CP00-125 section, so the docs read as if CP00-125 hands off to itself at S11. The authoritative code/contract are correct: PERMISSION_KERNEL_CP125_PACK_BINDING.next_pack_id='CP00-126', next_subphase_id='RP02.P07.M03.S21' (failure-taxonomy-risk-boundary.js) and contract failure_taxonomy_risk_boundary_pack.next_pack_id='CP00-126'.
findings.recommendation: Keep the 'Handoff is fixed to CP00-125 / RP02.P07.M03.S11' bullet under the CP00-124 section and add a CP00-125 handoff bullet pointing to CP00-126 / RP02.P07.M03.S21. Documentation-only; does not affect runtime, contract, or no-write guarantees.

parsed_review.recommendation: Approve CP00-125 closeout. The pack closes all 10 planned Risk A failure-taxonomy units (RP02.P07.M03.S11-S20), inherits CP124 behavior, and preserves synthetic-only/metadata-only no-write boundaries. The single P3 is a README-only inaccuracy that may be corrected opportunistically and is not a closeout blocker.
parsed_review.rationale: Scope verification against the staged diff: (1) All 10 deliverables are present and typed in CP125_UNITS — lock conflict (S11), retry exhaustion (S12), rollback expectation (S13), compensation expectation (S14), blocked-claim receipt (S15), failure fixture (S16), failure unit test (S17), failure integration smoke (S18), audit failure hint (S19), Hermes failure evidence (S20); deliverable counts (failure_recovery 3, implementation 1, hermes_evidence 2, fixture 1, test 2, security_audit 1) match manifest and validator. (2) CP124 inheritance is wired via imports of PERMISSION_KERNEL_CP124_PERMISSION_FIXTURE_FAILURE_TAXONOMY_CONTRACT and runPermissionKernelCp124...Case, asserting inherited_failure_taxonomy_result_count=61, lock status 'blocked_lock_conflict', retry status 'retry_exhausted_no_retry_execution'. (3) freezeNoWriteResult forces all execution/write flags false AFTER spreading inputs, so no case can override them; lock_policy.lock_acquired/lock_token_persisted=false, retry_policy.retry_executed=false, rollback_policy/compensation_policy.*_executed=false with *_expected isolated inside policy objects (no top-level execution). (4) blocked_claim_receipt.preview_only=true & emitted_to_hermes_runtime=false; audit_failure_hint.emitted_to_audit_ledger=false & hidden_fields_rendered=false; hermes_failure_evidence.reference_only=true & writes_hermes_runtime=false. (5) failure_fixture.contains_real_data=false, failure_test_binding.deterministic=true & runs_against_synthetic_metadata_only=true; replay anchors/command_anchors are inert static strings, never executed (no fs/db/network/route code in the new module). (6) No runtime UI/API route, policy mutation, audit/product/database write, idempotency persistence, lock acquisition, retry/rollback/compensation execution, external share/export, AI/analytics, LDIP, or HRX split is added — runtime_*_route_added=false, hrx_embedded_boundary='embedded_people_hr_evidence_module_inside_law_firm_os', and the full no_write_attestation (19 flags) is false in both source and contract. (7) Contract/exports/hidden-fields/validator are mutually consistent (11 public_exports match the module's exports; current_pack flips to CP00-125 risk A, 10 units, flag 'permission_kernel_failure_taxonomy_risk_boundary_verified'); index.js re-export and the validation script's CP125 assertions align with the source. Note: the inherited CP124 values (61, the two case IDs/statuses) and plan-binding (closeout-pack-plan CP00-125 entry / cp00-125 manifest) live outside this diff and are assumed present/correct; both are self-checked by the validator and tests, so divergence would fail closeout rather than slip through. No P0/P1/P2 issues and no security concerns (no real client data, no injection sink, hidden_source_fields are names-only used for negative assertions).

raw_result.result:
Read-only review complete. The CP00-125 diff is a clean, additive, metadata-only continuation of the established RP02 pattern; all no-write/boundary guarantees hold. One low-severity README documentation inconsistency. Verdict below.
